summary: R9 read-only data repo hygiene/git sync/privacy review completed. The revised unfinished-work document is adequate from this lens because it treats public visibility as the default risk model, blocks or local-only classifies raw transcripts and private artifacts, requires shared path classification across public-data safety/git_sync/auto_sync, and separates dirty/degraded data state from final completion claims.
decisions: Observed public-data safety check: degraded with zero blockers and many warnings; this matches the document requirement that warnings/dirty state cannot be hidden in final readiness.
Observed data hook verifier passed, supporting the document statement that data git hooks have a verification surface.
next_steps: Implement the P1-F shared classification table and hard-gate dirty/untracked data state classes before any release or push readiness claim.
